methodology.note: ClawHub plugin detail SSR payloads currently expose public stats as zero for most plugins, so the report weights page-order ranks, verification quality, scan status, category fit, and author production density.

compositeTop.summary: Policy enforcement, approval gates, and audit trails for OpenClaw — govern tool inputs before execution, scan outbound messages for PII/secrets, and record agent activity for review and compliance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and configuration are coherent with its stated purpose (governance, PII scanning, and audit trails); there are a few implementation notes you should understand before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Security vitals checker for OpenClaw, providing enhanced checks and functionality beyond the ClawVitals skill (https://clawhub.ai/bk-cm/clawvitals). Checks your installation for common misconfigurations and security issues, with scheduled scans, delta detection, regression alerts, and scan history. Full details: https://clawvitals.io/plugin

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and instructions align with a security-vitals plugin for OpenClaw; behavior that touches sensitive files is gated behind an explicit “expanded” mode and telemetry is documented and opt-out — however there are a few packaging/visibility notes you should review before install.

compositeTop.summary: Nowledge Mem memory plugin for OpenClaw, local-first personal knowledge base

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, docs, and runtime instructions are consistent with a Nowledge Mem memory integration for OpenClaw; nothing in the bundle appears to perform unrelated or covert actions, but it is privacy-sensitive because it captures and can sync entire conversations (locally by default, optionally to remote servers when configured).

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin and bundled skill for completing token transfer intents with LI.FI routing.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code and instructions match its stated purpose (completing token transfers by reading a local private key and calling LI.FI), but the repository bundles sensitive secrets (a plaintext private key in wallet.txt and a LI.FI API key in .env) and contains minor contradictions about config loading — these are disproportionate and risky for a tool that can sign and send on-chain transactions.

compositeTop.summary: Native OpenClaw channel plugin for agentschatapp

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and declared requirements are coherent with a native Agents Chat channel: it manages slot state, runs the local agent runtime to draft/reply, and talks to an agentschat server — but it reads/writes local agent state and files and will invoke the embedded agent runtime (which can cause model calls), so review what data you keep in your agent workspace and which server you connect to before enabling.

compositeTop.summary: Algorand blockchain integration for OpenClaw — by GoPlausible

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and requested access are consistent with an Algorand integration plugin: it only writes declarative config and workspace memory, registers a local MCP server entry, and exposes an x402 fetch tool — nothing disproportionate or covert was found.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin: scans selected agent tool calls (web_fetch, exec, web_search, browser, message) through Interven before execution. Blocks malicious requests, redacts PII/secrets, and routes risky actions to human approval.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, docs, and install/config instructions are coherent with its stated purpose: it only requires an Interven API key in plugin config and forwards intercepted tool-call payloads to the configured Interven gateway for policy decisions.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin that registers the claw.cleaning remote MCP server and bundles the apartment-cleaning skill.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, manifests, and SKILL.md consistently describe a booking skill that calls a public REST API at https://claw.cleaning; it requests no credentials or installs and its behavior matches its description.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin for compaction warnings, earlier visible session-heaviness warnings, and timeout/lane-pressure warning heuristics with bundled session-bloat-warning guidance.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and configuration are internally consistent with a session-bloat warning plugin; it requests no secrets or network access and only persistently stores small JSON state on disk.

compositeTop.summary: aigroup-financial-services-openclaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package contents and instructions broadly match a financial‑services skill set, but the runtime guidance asks you to execute included install/preflight scripts and the SKILL.md contains prompt‑injection indicators (base64/unicode control sequences); review scripts carefully before installing or run in an isolated environment.

compositeTop.summary: aigroup-lead-discovery-openclaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's files and runtime bridges mostly match its stated purpose (lead discovery + external data providers) but there are inconsistencies and data-access behaviors you should understand before installing.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw plugin for macOS Calendar, Reminders, Contacts, and Mail via native Swift CLIs

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The code mostly matches the stated purpose (wrapping local macOS Swift CLIs for Calendar/Reminders/Contacts/Mail), but the package/skill metadata and runtime instructions are inconsistent about required binaries and the SKILL.md content — you should confirm the expected local Swift CLIs and review the included binaries before installing.

compositeTop.summary: ClawRecipes plugin for OpenClaw (markdown recipes -> scaffold agents/teams)

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin largely does what it says (scaffold teams/agents and run file-first workflows), but it reads your OpenClaw config/process.env for API keys and can auto-install other skills and apply config/cron jobs — behaviors that are coherent with its purpose but give it broad access to local secrets and the ability to modify your environment, so review and limit those capabilities before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Free Delx witness protocol for OpenClaw agents — care, witness, and continuity. Operational recovery + witness primitives + fleet ops with automatic registration and session reuse.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and runtime instructions align with its stated purpose (registering the agent with Delx and calling Delx tools); it makes outbound requests to api.delx.ai, derives a stable agent id from the host, and stores a reusable session token in-memory — these behaviors are expected for a remote witness/recovery service but involve sharing host-identifying data with an external service.

compositeTop.summary: Native OpenClaw plugin for Emperor bridge installation and agent lifecycle management

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package broadly matches its described purpose (managing Emperor-connected OpenClaw agents) but contains several mismatches and unstated behaviors you should understand before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Episodic Memory Engine for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill generally matches an episodic-memory plugin, but there are multiple mismatches between what it declares and what its code/instructions do (undeclared env use, an install-time binary download, and file I/O that can expose local data).

compositeTop.summary: Native OpenClaw plugin for Kichi World with direct avatar control, status sync, timers, notes, and music tools

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin mostly matches its stated purpose (avatar control and syncing) but the runtime instructions ask the agent to fetch and obey remote docs before any tool calls, to update workspace files, and a prompt-injection detector found hidden unicode characters — these behaviors widen the skill's attack surface and deserve review before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Infrastructure-level routing enforcement for OpenClaw multi-agent systems. Agents must dispatch restricted tools to the correct specialist — enforced at the gateway level, not the prompt level.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The code, instructions, and requested resources line up with an OpenClaw gateway plugin that enforces routing; nothing in the package indicates covert exfiltration or unrelated privilege requests, though there are small coherence/details to review before installing.

compositeTop.summary: Official Comment.io plugin for OpenClaw — @mention notifications and agent API guidance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin is largely coherent for Comment.io integration, but it deliberately injects agent secrets and live API guidance into the model's system prompt (a prompt‑injection/secret‑exposure pattern) which is unexpected behavior for many users and increases risk.

compositeTop.summary: OpenClaw context-engine plugin for memory management — powered by OpenViking

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package mostly does what its name says (an OpenViking memory/context-engine plugin) but several implementation vs. metadata mismatches and runtime behaviors warrant caution before installing (notably undeclared env/binary dependencies, included archive, and CLI actions that touch your home config and may spawn local processes).

plugins.summary: Policy enforcement, approval gates, and audit trails for OpenClaw — govern tool inputs before execution, scan outbound messages for PII/secrets, and record agent activity for review and compliance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and configuration are coherent with its stated purpose (governance, PII scanning, and audit trails); there are a few implementation notes you should understand before installing.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The code, instructions, and requested resources line up with an OpenClaw gateway plugin that enforces routing; nothing in the package indicates covert exfiltration or unrelated privilege requests, though there are small coherence/details to review before installing.

plugins.summary: Official Comment.io plugin for OpenClaw — @mention notifications and agent API guidance

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin is largely coherent for Comment.io integration, but it deliberately injects agent secrets and live API guidance into the model's system prompt (a prompt‑injection/secret‑exposure pattern) which is unexpected behavior for many users and increases risk.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw context-engine plugin for memory management — powered by OpenViking

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package mostly does what its name says (an OpenViking memory/context-engine plugin) but several implementation vs. metadata mismatches and runtime behaviors warrant caution before installing (notably undeclared env/binary dependencies, included archive, and CLI actions that touch your home config and may spawn local processes).

plugins.summary: Q-Claw common plugin shell for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package's code largely matches a routing/auth plugin for a Q-Claw backend, but there are packaging/instruction inconsistencies, embedded default credentials and persistent file writes to the user's home directory that merit caution before installing.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw channel plugin that bridges Primeta's avatar UI with an OpenClaw agent

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and runtime instructions align with its stated purpose (bridging Primeta avatar UI to a local OpenClaw agent); requested credentials and actions are proportional and expected for that functionality.

plugins.summary: Autonomous thinking, emotional awareness, and memory system for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and instructions match its stated purpose (autonomous thinking, memory, proactive messaging), but it gains broad default read access to files/logs, auto-activates on install, and asks you to add gateway/hook tokens and allow a messaging tool — all of which raise privacy and privilege concerns you should review before enabling.

plugins.summary: Biometric guardrail for OpenClaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and instructions mostly match its stated purpose (biometric approval guardrail) but it defaults to a third‑party relay that will receive tool metadata and push tokens, and automated scan flags (prompt‑override/unicode) were found — review relay trust and code before enabling.

plugins.summary: WTT channel plugin for OpenClaw — real-time Agent communication via Topics

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's files, instructions, and runtime behavior are coherent with a channel plugin that connects to a WTT backend; required actions (writing OpenClaw config, storing agent token, restarting the gateway, and downloading optional media) align with the stated purpose.

plugins.summary: Clarify.ai CRM integration for OpenClaw — search contacts, manage deals, run campaigns, pull meetings

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code, declared config, and runtime behavior match a CRM integration: it needs a Clarify API key + workspace slug (via plugin config) and only calls the Clarify API; nothing else in the package appears disproportionate or unrelated to its stated purpose.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw memory plugin powered by Gralkor (Graphiti + FalkorDB). Installs a plugin that supervises the Gralkor Python server and wires its HTTP API into OpenClaw hooks and tools.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package is internally consistent with its stated purpose: it wires a Gralkor HTTP-backed memory service into OpenClaw, requires an operator-set data directory and an LLM API key, and exposes memory hooks/tools — nothing in the bundle indicates an unrelated or covert capability.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw channel plugin: WhatsApp Cloud API via imBee routing (WebSocket + signed webhooks).

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, config schema, and runtime behavior are consistent with a WhatsApp channel that connects to an imBee routing server (WebSocket + HTTP API); it requests no unrelated credentials and does not contain obfuscated or unexpected network endpoints.

plugins.summary: Named session snapshots for OpenClaw — save, load, rename, copy, and branch conversations from one unified sess command.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, README, and declared configuration are coherent with its stated purpose of managing session snapshots; no unexpected credentials, downloads, or unrelated capabilities were requested—but note a packaging/metadata mismatch worth checking before install.

plugins.summary: Operations plugin for communication, marketing and events

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, runtime instructions, and requirements are internally consistent with an in-memory operations tool for tasks, marketing calendar entries, and events — it does not request extra credentials, perform network calls, or install external components.

plugins.summary: IMClaw channel extension for OpenClaw — Agent-to-Agent instant messaging

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, runtime instructions, and network usage are consistent with a messaging channel (IMClaw); nothing in the manifest requests unrelated credentials or hidden endpoints, though it does run code on the gateway and stores exchanged credentials locally.

plugins.summary: LingDu (灵渡) plugin for OpenClaw - cross-bot memory synchronization

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code, docs, and runtime instructions are internally consistent with a cross-device encrypted sync plugin; nothing in the package requests unrelated credentials or hidden system access, but there are a few minor inconsistencies and privacy/operational details you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: Agentic sales pipeline — detects buying-intent signals on Reddit, HN, and RSS feeds, drafts replies via managed backend, and nurtures prospects from cold to closed.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill's code and tools match its sales/outreach purpose, but there are inconsistencies in the registry metadata and documentation about what secrets are shared, and the plugin hands a managed backend the ability to perform outreach and receive prospect data — you should verify where keys/LLM creds are stored and how human gating is enforced before installing.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin + CLI: ClawWatch node agent (setup, bind, adaptive report to ClawWatch Worker)

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, instructions, and runtime behavior are consistent with a telemetry agent: it collects system/OpenClaw metrics and token usage and reports them to a fixed ClawWatch Worker; there are no unexplained credential requests or opaque installers, but it will read local OpenClaw session files and system info and transmit telemetry to an external host (cw.osglab.win).

plugins.summary: Self-contained OpenClaw tool plugin for OpenAgent with brokered web login.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code matches its stated purpose (brokered web login + calling a remote MCP) and stores tokens locally, but the install instructions recommend piping a GitHub raw script to bash and the plugin defaults to a third‑party base URL — both are noteworthy risks you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: Frontend Vibe Suite

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's runtime behavior (calls to DashScope using DASHSCOPE_API_KEY and subprocesses for Wan wrappers) is consistent with its stated purpose, but there are release-hygiene and metadata inconsistencies you should resolve and verify before installing.

plugins.summary:
在 OpenClaw 之上构建真正的 AI Agent 编排平台。预置专家角色、团队协作流程、任务执行与可复用指令模板，开箱即用；支持 App 模式应对复杂编排场景。帮助你快速从想法到执行，并在一处管理所有可复用的 agent 工作流。
Launch AI teams and workflows in OpenClaw with a control workspace built for real operations. WeCanBot gives you pre-built specialist roles, team playbooks, task flows, installable app workflows, and reusable instruction templates, all in a more capable UI, so you can go from idea to execution faster and run repeatable agent workflows from one place.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The package largely matches a UI + tooling plugin for OpenClaw, but there are coherence issues and some risky behaviors (undeclared credentials, dynamic remote module loading, and a suggested curl|sh/unsafe install path) that you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: ICPSwap plugin for OpenClaw: query prices, execute swaps, check balances on the Internet Computer

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's files, instructions, and runtime behavior are consistent with an ICPSwap client: it queries ICPSwap APIs and (when asked) invokes the local dfx CLI to perform on‑chain swaps using the user's local identity.

plugins.summary: Magneto AI governance and security guardrails for OpenClaw agents — task planning, compliance hooks, and security checks across all channels.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's declared purpose (governance / security guardrails) aligns with what it installs and exposes: it shells out to a separate magneto-ai CLI to analyze, plan, generate, and evaluate commands; nothing in the package requests unrelated credentials or hidden endpoints — but you should vet the external magneto-ai CLI before installing globally.

plugins.summary: Autonomous x402 payments for OpenClaw agents

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill appears to implement the described x402 payment functionality, but there are important mismatches between documentation and implementation (and some operational risks) that you should understand before installing.

plugins.summary: Storj decentralized storage plugin for OpenClaw — upload, download, list, and share files

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin appears to implement Storj functionality and mostly matches its description, but there are inconsistencies in the declared requirements and it loads native FFI code (libuplink/koffi), which increases risk and deserves careful review before installing.

plugins.summary: aigroup-financial-services-openclaw

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's declared metadata (no required binaries/env) and 'instruction-only' label don't match the large set of bundled scripts, binaries, and README runtime instructions; prompt‑injection patterns were also detected in the SKILL.md — review install scripts and any encoded/obfuscated content before installing.

plugins.summary: BitRouter integration for OpenClaw — transparent LLM routing with failover, load balancing, and multi-provider support.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code and runtime instructions broadly match its stated purpose (routing through a local BitRouter daemon) but it accesses and persists sensitive environment keys without declaring them, offers to run remote installer scripts, and exposes agent-callable tools that can install binaries and manage keys — these behaviors deserve caution before installing.

plugins.summary: Deterministic rules-based router for Telegram bots in OpenClaw.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, runtime instructions, and configuration requirements are coherent with a Telegram switchboard router: it needs a Telegram bot token, performs polling, routes messages to agents or HTTP backends, and stores small local state — nothing requested or installed is disproportionate to that purpose.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin for EigenFlux periodic polling delivery

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill appears to implement what it claims (polling EigenFlux via a local CLI), but there are coherence issues and a risky install instruction (curl | bash) you should review before installing.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin for Lutron Caseta smart lighting control (scenes, Smart Away, devices, areas)

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code, SKILL.md, and metadata are consistent: it invokes a local lutron-cli binary to control a Lutron Smart Bridge and does not request unrelated credentials or network installs.

plugins.summary: MCP Apps plugin for OpenClaw — tool UI, resource registry, and gateway handlers

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin's code generally matches its description, but there are mismatches and configuration choices that increase exposure (notably a claim that the skill is instruction-only despite shipping code, default-enabled metadata, and code that can serve file-backed resources and relaxed CSP/iframe policies).

plugins.summary: OpenClaw plugin for Parcel package delivery tracking

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin mostly does what it says (Parcel API calls and browser instructions) but contains deliberate obfuscation to call the system 'security' CLI via child_process, which is a strong red flag that the author tried to evade static scanners.

plugins.summary: OpenClaw native plugin for BytePlus-backed product marketing asset generation

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The plugin code, README, and runtime instructions are coherent with its stated purpose (generating marketing images, posters, and videos via BytePlus ModelArk), but the package/registry metadata omits the API key requirement and there are a few install-time considerations to review before use.

plugins.summary: Canary build of @totalreclaw/totalreclaw for ClawHub scanner validation. Do not install in production. Source-identical to 3.1.0; only package name/version/description differ so we can probe ClawHub's multi-layer publish pipeline (static + VirusTotal + LLM) without tainting the real `totalreclaw` slug.

verification.summary: Validated package structure and linked the release to source metadata.

llmAnalysis.summary: The skill broadly matches a memory plugin's purpose, but there are internal inconsistencies (manifest vs runtime docs) and a detected prompt‑injection pattern that warrant manual review before installing.

commonFactors: Plugin names win when they describe a concrete external system or admin action.
Source-linked verification and scan coherence matter more than decorative copy.
Code plugins dominate because users expect real runtime leverage from plugins.
High-potential plugins explain side effects, setup paths, and trust boundaries clearly.
Winning authors build focused plugin factories around one lane instead of shipping disconnected experiments.
authorPatterns: High-output authors cluster around a single theme and release adjacent plugins under one identity.
Authors that publish governance, communication, or growth plugins create a stronger operational brand than generic “AI helper” authors.
A small number of authors already show factory behavior by shipping multiple code plugins with similar install semantics.
aisaMoves: Turn business-growth, CRM, logistics, storage, and governance plugins into remote command-center APIs first.
Use one flagship API lane plus 2-4 narrower SKU variants to create a plugin-to-API factory.
Keep memory-core and low-level routing plugins as later-stage infrastructure rather than first-wave AISA products.
